distribution: GameAnalysis lightweight repository may exclude tools/, but agents should automatically restore trusted third-party tools when needed and persist them under tools/.

tools.name: AssetRipper
tools.purpose: Unity project reconstruction and full local export from user-authorized game folders.

tools.name: UABEA
tools.purpose: Fallback Unity asset bundle inspection when AssetRipper or UnityPy cannot read a specific container.

tools.name: UnityPy
tools.purpose: Scripted extraction of readable Unity Sprite, Texture2D, TextAsset, AudioClip, and animation-frame data.

tools.name: pytest
tools.purpose: Run GameAnalysis Skill regression tests through scripts/run_skill_tests.ps1 using the bundled Python environment.

tools.name: repak
tools.purpose: Read-only Unreal .pak info/list/get workflows for user-authorized packaged games before any optional asset export.

tools.name: UEViewer / UModel
tools.purpose: Optional Unreal package viewer/exporter for small verified samples. Large commercial packages may require engine version parameters or time out; record failures.

tools.name: UndertaleModCli
tools.purpose: Automated local reading and exporter-script execution for user-authorized GameMaker data.win files.

tools.name: UndertaleModTool
tools.purpose: GUI cross-checker and source of bundled GameMaker Resource Exporters scripts such as ExportAllSprites, ExportAllTextures, ExportAllSounds, and ExportAllStringsJSON.

tools.name: ARM ASTC Encoder / astcenc
tools.purpose: Decode Cocos Creator and other mobile ASTC textures to local PNG previews for user-authorized study outputs. This local copy is the official windows-x64 release for x64 Windows; still record URL, version, license, SHA256, and -version/-help output in each current study-root.

tools.name: Stexatlaser / Stex
tools.purpose: Default Klei TEX/KTEX to PNG converter for Don't Starve Together and similar Klei resources.

tools.name: DstAnimTool
tools.local_patch: Python 3 compatibility: write XML as UTF-8 text, decode bytes hash strings, and preserve unknown facing bitmasks as _facing_<hex> suffixes.
tools.purpose: Decompile Klei/DST anim.bin and build.bin files into XML for animation structure analysis.

tools.name: python-fsb5
tools.purpose: Primary FSB5 sample extractor for Klei/DST FMOD banks. The Windows release supplies libogg/libvorbis DLLs needed for Vorbis sample rebuilding.

tools.name: vgmstream CLI
tools.purpose: Fallback decoder for FSB banks that python-fsb5 cannot parse; used to export all subsongs to WAV.

tools.name: KleiTEXConverter
tools.status: Downloaded but not used by default because this host lacks Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App 5.0.0; Stex is the CLI default.
tools.purpose: GUI/batch fallback for Klei TEX conversion when the required .NET Desktop 5 runtime is available.

tools.name: ktools mirror bundle
tools.license: GPL lineage per nsimplex/ktools; mirror binary provenance should be rechecked before redistribution.
tools.status: Downloaded for Klei TEX/animation fallback, but ktech.exe and krane.exe exit -1073741515 on this host due to missing native DLL dependencies.
tools.purpose: Candidate fallback for KTEX PNG conversion and Klei animation to Spriter/SCML when a runnable build is available.

tools.name: Electron gallery viewer template
tools.source: Local template generated from earlier verified gallery viewer workflows.
tools.purpose: Legacy-compatible template for historical HTML gallery workflows; not the default image gallery path. New studies use GameImageGallery with per-project BAT, manifest, thumbnails, and error logs.

tools.name: GameImageGallery
tools.source: Local fixed Electron image gallery built from assets/image-gallery-viewer-src.
tools.license: Internal GameAnalysis tool bundle; Electron runtime follows upstream Electron licensing.
tools.purpose: Open GameAnalysis study-root image manifests through a fixed local Windows EXE. Each project generates only a BAT launcher, manifest, thumbnails, and error logs.

tools.name: GameModelViewer
tools.source: Local Electron/Three.js model viewer built from assets/model-gallery-viewer-src.
tools.license: Local wrapper; Electron MIT; Three.js MIT
tools.purpose: Offline local preview of GameAnalysis exported glTF/GLB/FBX models. Each project generates only a BAT launcher, manifest, thumbs, preview-cache, and error logs.

tools.name: GameVfxViewer
tools.source: Local fixed Electron Unity VFX viewer built from assets/vfx-viewer-src.
tools.license: Local wrapper; Electron runtime follows upstream Electron licensing.
tools.purpose: Offline local preview of GameAnalysis Unity VFX manifests and best-effort reconstructed ParticleSystem, TrailRenderer, and LineRenderer data. Each project generates only a BAT launcher, manifest, reconstructed JSON, preview-cache, unity-export, and error/status records.

tools.name: ILSpy / ilspycmd
tools.purpose: Check whether a Unity Assembly-CSharp.dll contains real managed IL method bodies or only dummy/stub signatures.

tools.name: Il2CppDumper
tools.purpose: Generate dump.cs, script.json, il2cpp.h, stringliteral.json, and DummyDll from GameAssembly.dll plus global-metadata.dat.

tools.name: Cpp2IL
tools.purpose: Optional IL2CPP analysis fallback. It may fail on newer metadata versions; record the failure instead of replacing Il2CppDumper.

tools.name: Ghidra
tools.purpose: Analyze GameAssembly.dll native code and apply Il2CppDumper mappings to inspect behavior relevant to art, animation, UI, VFX, camera, and audio.

tools.name: JDK
tools.purpose: Portable Java runtime used by bundled Ghidra without changing the system Java installation.

tools.name: IDA / IDA Free
tools.purpose: Optional manual cross-check for native code. IDA Free is not the default automated route because licensing and automation capabilities are limited.

boundary: These entries describe expected local paths and trusted sources for user-authorized learning workflows. The public repository may omit tools/; when network is available, missing tools must be restored automatically from trusted sources with license checks, source URL, version, hash, and help/version output, then persisted under tools/. Do not use them to distribute third-party original assets.
